title: You are Here where is here
display_title: You are Here where is here
wikitext:
[]: You are Here.... where is here?             Quote Post by Animejunkie » Sun Jan 09, 2011 10:37 am Okay so I've been thinking about NERV headquarters as a whole and just how big it actually is. And this thought came to my mind...In which headquarters would you rather be lost in... NERV, or Black Mesa (for those of you who dont know what black mesa is gtfo =P)... but seriously both these places are HUGE and they just seem so poorly constructed to actually help people get around... Me id rather be lost in black mesa just cause i feel more at home there =D

[]:             Quote Post by Oz » Sun Jan 09, 2011 10:40 am When I saw the topic's name, I thought this was going to be yet another attempt at solipsism that would be doomed to fail by definition.

[]:             Quote Post by Hawq » Sun Jan 09, 2011 10:53 am Silly question, Nerv of course I aint gonna wander round where a headcrab can get me (rebuild shows us that while giant headcrabs may exist in the Evangelion world their preferred prey is angels). Being lost in Nerv HQ also brings the possibility of running into some rather nice totty, either someone who knows where they are going or Misato who would likely also be lost, pretty sure could find something to do if we have to wait for someone to come get us

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Sun Jan 09, 2011 11:13 am Oz wrote: When I saw the topic's name, I thought this was going to be yet another attempt at solipsism that would be doomed to fail by definition.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Sun Jan 09, 2011 1:04 pm The maps are probably much easier to understand than Misato makes them out to be. She probably just sucks at directions.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 09, 2011 2:28 pm This thread is going to spark several NGE/HL fanfics. I don't think Nerv HQ is quite as large and complicated as the Black Mesa complex. Apart from episode one, everything seems easy to get to. The only time anybody seems to have trouble getting around is in episode 11 when the electricity went out.

[]:             Quote Post by Galaxy News Reidio » Sun Jan 09, 2011 2:44 pm Sachi wrote: This thread is going to spark several NGE/HL fanfics.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Sun Jan 09, 2011 10:01 pm I'd like to be lost in NERV HQ, That way Ritsuko can come find me and then we can get lost again in a broom closet. (sorry for my outburst, women that are better than me get me excited.)

[]:             Quote Post by bigbadtyrant » Sun Jan 09, 2011 10:25 pm Id rather be lost in NERV HQ becuase black mesa has those annoying headcrabs that piss me off all day, that game makes me rage hard :/

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Mon Jan 10, 2011 12:35 am Sachi wrote: This thread is going to spark several NGE/HL fanfics.

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Mon Jan 10, 2011 1:18 am Well, let's compare the sizes of each facility. Approximate size of Tokyo-3 The facility is only a fraction the size of the mesa itself. Though Lilith's egg is about the same size as Black Mesa, NERV is much smaller, as they haven't dug out the entire egg. It's basically the geofront, the bridge, the cadges, testing facilities, service corridors, parking, the main shaft, and terminal dogma. Easy. I'd rather get lost there than an area potentially bigger than new york city. I'd like to see some fanart of an Evangelion with an enormous headcrab attached to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Jan 10, 2011 5:44 pm [s:2vdv15m0]FUCKING HEAD CRABS! DIE MOTHERFUCKERS![/s:2vdv15m0] Eh...Nerv of course. At least the place had adequate lighting and doubles as a modeling agency.

[]:             Quote Post by bigbadtyrant » Mon Jan 10, 2011 6:17 pm @ Trajan: true that @ Merridian: i wonder how a headcrab would be able to get that big anyway

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Mon Jan 10, 2011 6:19 pm Merridian wrote: I'd like to see some fanart of an Evangelion with an enormous headcrab attached to it.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Jan 10, 2011 6:23 pm What would be really funny is if you had a Shinji with a headcrab with a caption that says, "Woman, you gave me crabs!" Extra points if the headcrab has Asuka's face just for lolz.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Mon Jan 10, 2011 7:59 pm 'Shinji got a message from his father that said that aliens and monsters were attacking his place'.

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Mon Jan 10, 2011 11:22 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: What would be really funny is if you had a Shinji with a headcrab with a caption that says, "Woman, you gave me crabs!" Extra points if the headcrab has Asuka's face just for lolz.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Mon Jan 10, 2011 11:55 pm Trajan wrote: I think I laughed so hard I cried a little. Someone has to make that image now.

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Sat Jan 15, 2011 1:48 pm This thread makes me wanna play some HL :S I'd like to visit both: Though before Black Mesa got infested by aliens and then the HECU came in slaughtering personnel and Xen lifeforms and then finally nuked. Though before Nerv HQ was attacked by angels continously and then by the JSSDF who came in slaughtering personnel, and then finally destroyed in 3rd Impact. What about Aperture Laboratories? Oh wait, deadly neurotoxins...

[]:             Quote Post by Norsehound » Sat Jan 29, 2011 5:36 pm I always presumed Black Mesa ended up confusing and convoluted because parts of the facility were added on at different times, resulting in a hodge-podge assembly of different places from different eras. After all, Black Mesa was originally a cold war missile silo complex. Most of Gordon's adventures go through the older, industrial parts of the facility (and through several silos). What's topside probably looks a lot like NERV. NERV, though, I thought was deliberately constructed to be confusing from the ground up. I also got the sense that it was bigger than just the cages, geofront, command base, et all. It's also got what's left of all the labs and equipment from the development of Project-E. Most of it (like the graveyard) is derelict by the time of the series, or conducting other low-level experiments (for extra funding?). Here's a thought- instead of turning everyone into orange goo and meshing all humankind's souls together into a gigantic tree thing, the Third Impact opens up a portal to Xen. The second impact shipped the south pole TO Zen but SEELE wants to try again to get it right.
